# Gluetun WebUI A lightweight web UI for monitoring and controlling [Gluetun](https://github.com/qdm12/gluetun) — the VPN client container for Docker. ![Status: Connected](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-connected-brightgreen) ![Node 25](https://img.shields.io/badge/node-25--alpine-blue) ![Docker](https://img.shields.io/badge/docker-compose-blue)

# Gluetun WebUI Uma interface web leve para monitorar e controlar o [Gluetun](https://github.com/qdm12/gluetun) — o container cliente VPN para Docker. ![Status: Conectado](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-connected-brightgreen) ![Node 25](https://img.shields.io/badge/node-25--alpine-blue) ![Docker](https://img.shields.io/badge/docker-compose-blue)

RowTranslations.originContent: A lightweight web UI for monitoring and controlling [Gluetun](https://github.com/qdm12/gluetun) — the VPN client container for Docker.
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Uma interface web leve para monitorar e controlar o [Gluetun](https://github.com/qdm12/gluetun) — o container cliente VPN para Docker.

Content:
---

## Features

- ✨ **Multi-VPN Support** — Monitor & control up to 20 Gluetun instances simultaneously
- Live VPN status banner (connected / paused / disconnected)
- Public exit IP, country, region, city, and organisation
- VPN provider, protocol (WireGuard / OpenVPN), server details
- Port forwarding and DNS status
- Start / Stop VPN controls
- Auto-refresh with configurable interval (5s – 60s)
- Last 30 poll ticks colour-coded in history bar
- Responsive design (mobile, tablet, desktop)

---

## Screenshots
![alt text](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Sir-Scuzza/gluetun-webui/main/image-1.png)

---

## Requirements

- Docker + Docker Compose
- Gluetun running with its HTTP control server enabled (default port `8000`)
- Gluetun and gluetun-webui on the same Docker network

> Supports `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` (works on Mac Intel/Apple Silicon, Linux, and Windows).

---

## Quick Start

### Option A1: Single Instance (Recommended)

Add `gluetun-webui` to your existing compose file alongside Gluetun:
Translation:
---

## Funcionalidades

- ✨ **Suporte Multi-VPN** — Monitore e controle até 20 instâncias Gluetun simultaneamente
- Banner de status VPN ao vivo (conectado / pausado / desconectado)
- IP público de saída, país, região, cidade e organização
- Provedor de VPN, protocolo (WireGuard / OpenVPN), detalhes do servidor
- Status de encaminhamento de portas e DNS
- Controles para iniciar / parar VPN
- Atualização automática com intervalo configurável (5s – 60s)
- Últimos 30 ciclos de consulta codificados por cor na barra de histórico
- Design responsivo (mobile, tablet, desktop)

---

## Capturas de Tela
![texto alternativo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Sir-Scuzza/gluetun-webui/main/image-1.png)

---

## Requisitos

- Docker + Docker Compose
- Gluetun executando com seu servidor de controle HTTP habilitado (porta padrão `8000`)
- Gluetun e gluetun-webui na mesma rede Docker

> Suporta `linux/amd64` e `linux/arm64` (funciona em Mac Intel/Apple Silicon, Linux e Windows).

---

## Início Rápido

### Opção A1: Instância Única (Recomendado)

Adicione `gluetun-webui` ao seu arquivo compose existente juntamente com o Gluetun:

RowTranslations.originContent: - ✨ **Multi-VPN Support** — Monitor & control up to 20 Gluetun instances simultaneously
RowTranslations.translatedContent: - ✨ **Suporte Multi-VPN** — Monitore e controle até 20 instâncias Gluetun simultaneamente

RowTranslations.originContent: - Live VPN status banner (connected / paused / disconnected)
RowTranslations.translatedContent: - Banner de status VPN ao vivo (conectado / pausado / desconectado)

RowTranslations.originContent: - Public exit IP, country, region, city, and organisation
RowTranslations.translatedContent: - IP público de saída, país, região, cidade e organização

RowTranslations.originContent: - VPN provider, protocol (WireGuard / OpenVPN), server details
RowTranslations.translatedContent: - Provedor de VPN, protocolo (WireGuard / OpenVPN), detalhes do servidor
- Status de encaminhamento de portas e DNS

RowTranslations.originContent: - Last 30 poll ticks colour-coded in history bar
RowTranslations.translatedContent: - Últimos 30 ciclos de consulta codificados por cor na barra de histórico

RowTranslations.originContent: - Gluetun running with its HTTP control server enabled (default port `8000`)
RowTranslations.translatedContent: - Gluetun executando com seu servidor de controle HTTP habilitado (porta padrão `8000`)

RowTranslations.originContent: - Gluetun and gluetun-webui on the same Docker network
RowTranslations.translatedContent: - Gluetun e gluetun-webui na mesma rede Docker

RowTranslations.originContent: > Supports `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` (works on Mac Intel/Apple Silicon, Linux, and Windows).
RowTranslations.translatedContent: > Suporta `linux/amd64` e `linux/arm64` (funciona em Mac Intel/Apple Silicon, Linux e Windows).

RowTranslations.originContent: Add `gluetun-webui` to your existing compose file alongside Gluetun:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Adicione `gluetun-webui` ao seu arquivo compose existente juntamente com o Gluetun:

Content:
```yaml
gluetun-webui:
  image: scuzza/gluetun-webui:latest
  container_name: gluetun-webui
  ports:
    - "127.0.0.1:3000:3000"
  environment:
    - GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL=http://gluetun:8000
    # Uncomment if Gluetun auth is enabled:
    #- GLUETUN_API_KEY=yourtoken
    #- GLUETUN_USER=username
    #- GLUETUN_PASSWORD=password
  networks:
    - your_network_name
  restart: unless-stopped
  read_only: true
  tmpfs:
    - /tmp
  security_opt:
    - no-new-privileges:true
  cap_drop:
    - ALL
  healthcheck:
    test: ["CMD", "wget", "-qO-", "http://localhost:3000/api/health"]
    interval: 30s
    timeout: 5s
    start_period: 10s
    retries: 3
```
Translation:
```yaml
gluetun-webui:
  image: scuzza/gluetun-webui:latest
  container_name: gluetun-webui
  ports:
    - "127.0.0.1:3000:3000"
  environment:
    - GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL=http://gluetun:8000
    # Uncomment if Gluetun auth is enabled:
    #- GLUETUN_API_KEY=yourtoken
    #- GLUETUN_USER=username
    #- GLUETUN_PASSWORD=password
  networks:
    - your_network_name
  restart: unless-stopped
  read_only: true
  tmpfs:
    - /tmp
  security_opt:
    - no-new-privileges:true
  cap_drop:
    - ALL
  healthcheck:
    test: ["CMD", "wget", "-qO-", "http://localhost:3000/api/health"]
    interval: 30s
    timeout: 5s
    start_period: 10s
    retries: 3
```

RowTranslations.translatedContent: # Uncomment if Gluetun auth is enabled:

### Option A2: Multiple Instances

Monitor 2+ Gluetun instances with separate dashboards:
Translation:
### Opção A2: Múltiplas Instâncias

Monitore 2 ou mais instâncias do Gluetun com dashboards separados:

RowTranslations.originContent: Monitor 2+ Gluetun instances with separate dashboards:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Monitore 2 ou mais instâncias do Gluetun com dashboards separados:

Content:
```yaml
gluetun-webui:
  image: scuzza/gluetun-webui:latest
  container_name: gluetun-webui
  ports:
    - "127.0.0.1:3000:3000"
  environment:
    - GLUETUN_1_NAME=VPN - London
    - GLUETUN_1_URL=http://gluetun-1:8000
    - GLUETUN_1_API_KEY=token1
    
    - GLUETUN_2_NAME=VPN - Amsterdam  
    - GLUETUN_2_URL=http://gluetun-2:8000
    - GLUETUN_2_API_KEY=token2
    
    - GLUETUN_3_NAME=VPN - Singapore
    - GLUETUN_3_URL=http://gluetun-3:8000
    - GLUETUN_3_API_KEY=token3
  networks:
    - your_network_name
  restart: unless-stopped
  read_only: true
  tmpfs:
    - /tmp
  security_opt:
    - no-new-privileges:true
  cap_drop:
    - ALL
```

Content: ### Option B: Build Locally
Translation: ### Opção B: Construir Localmente

Content:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/Sir-Scuzza/gluetun-webui.git
cd gluetun-webui
docker compose up -d --build
```
Translation:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/Sir-Scuzza/gluetun-webui.git
cd gluetun-webui
docker compose up -d --build
```

Content: Then run (either option):

```bash
docker compose up -d
```
Translation:
```bash
docker compose up -d
```

Content:
The UI is available at **http://localhost:3000**

---

## Network Setup

Both Gluetun and gluetun-webui must be on the same Docker network so `http://gluetun:8000` resolves correctly.

**Same compose file** — just add both services to the same network (most common):
Translation:
A interface está disponível em **http://localhost:3000**

---

## Configuração de Rede

Tanto o Gluetun quanto o gluetun-webui devem estar na mesma rede Docker para que `http://gluetun:8000` seja resolvido corretamente.

**Mesmo arquivo compose** — basta adicionar ambos os serviços à mesma rede (mais comum):

RowTranslations.originContent: Both Gluetun and gluetun-webui must be on the same Docker network so `http://gluetun:8000` resolves correctly.
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Tanto o Gluetun quanto o gluetun-webui devem estar na mesma rede Docker para que `http://gluetun:8000` seja resolvido corretamente.

RowTranslations.originContent: **Same compose file** — just add both services to the same network (most common):
RowTranslations.translatedContent: **Mesmo arquivo compose** — basta adicionar ambos os serviços à mesma rede (mais comum):

Content:
```yaml
services:
  gluetun:
    networks:
      - arr-stack
  gluetun-webui:
    networks:
      - arr-stack

networks:
  arr-stack:
    driver: bridge
```
Translation:
```yaml
services:
  gluetun:
    networks:
      - arr-stack
  gluetun-webui:
    networks:
      - arr-stack

networks:
  arr-stack:
    driver: bridge
```

Content: **Separate compose files** — reference Gluetun's existing network as external. Find your network name with `docker network ls`:
Translation: **Arquivos compose separados** — referencie a rede existente do Gluetun como externa. Encontre o nome da sua rede com `docker network ls`:

RowTranslations.originContent: **Separate compose files** — reference Gluetun's existing network as external. Find your network name with `docker network ls`:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: **Arquivos compose separados** — referencie a rede existente do Gluetun como externa. Encontre o nome da sua rede com `docker network ls`:

Content:
```yaml
networks:
  ext-network:
    external: true
    name: your_gluetun_network_name
```
Translation:
```yaml
networks:
  ext-network:
    external: true
    name: your_gluetun_network_name
```

Content:
---

## Multi-VPN Support

### Multiple Instances

gluetun-webui supports monitoring and controlling **multiple Gluetun instances simultaneously**. Each instance displays as a separate dashboard in a responsive grid.

**Configuration**: Use numbered environment variables:
Translation:
---

## Suporte a Multi-VPN

### Múltiplas Instâncias

gluetun-webui suporta o monitoramento e controle de **múltiplas instâncias Gluetun simultaneamente**. Cada instância é exibida como um painel separado em uma grade responsiva.

**Configuração**: Use variáveis de ambiente numeradas:

RowTranslations.originContent: gluetun-webui supports monitoring and controlling **multiple Gluetun instances simultaneously**. Each instance displays as a separate dashboard in a responsive grid.
RowTranslations.translatedContent: gluetun-webui suporta o monitoramento e controle de **múltiplas instâncias Gluetun simultaneamente**. Cada instância é exibida como um painel separado em uma grade responsiva.

Content:
```yaml
gluetun-webui:
  image: scuzza/gluetun-webui:latest
  environment:
    # Instance 1
    - GLUETUN_1_NAME=VPN 1
    - GLUETUN_1_URL=http://gluetun-1:8000
    - GLUETUN_1_API_KEY=token1  # optional

    # Instance 2
    - GLUETUN_2_NAME=VPN 2
    - GLUETUN_2_URL=http://gluetun-2:8000
    - GLUETUN_2_API_KEY=token2  # optional

    # Instance 3
    - GLUETUN_3_NAME=VPN 3
    - GLUETUN_3_URL=http://gluetun-3:8000
    - GLUETUN_3_USER=admin
    - GLUETUN_3_PASSWORD=secret  # optional (HTTP Basic auth)
```
Translation:
```yaml
gluetun-webui:
  image: scuzza/gluetun-webui:latest
  environment:
    # Instance 1
    - GLUETUN_1_NAME=VPN 1
    - GLUETUN_1_URL=http://gluetun-1:8000
    - GLUETUN_1_API_KEY=token1  # optional

    # Instance 2
    - GLUETUN_2_NAME=VPN 2
    - GLUETUN_2_URL=http://gluetun-2:8000
    - GLUETUN_2_API_KEY=token2  # optional

    # Instance 3
    - GLUETUN_3_NAME=VPN 3
    - GLUETUN_3_URL=http://gluetun-3:8000
    - GLUETUN_3_USER=admin
    - GLUETUN_3_PASSWORD=secret  # optional (HTTP Basic auth)
```

Content:
**Supported**: Up to 20 instances (via `GLUETUN_1_URL` through `GLUETUN_20_URL`)  
**Responsive**: 1 full-width dashboard → 2 half-width → 3 third-width → 4 quarter-width → scrollable at 5+

### Backward Compatibility

If no numbered variables are configured, falls back to **legacy single-instance mode**:
Translation:
**Suportado**: Até 20 instâncias (via `GLUETUN_1_URL` até `GLUETUN_20_URL`)  
**Responsivo**: 1 dashboard largura total → 2 meia largura → 3 um terço da largura → 4 um quarto da largura → rolável a partir de 5+

### Compatibilidade Reversa

Se nenhuma variável numerada estiver configurada, retorna ao **modo legado de instância única**:

RowTranslations.originContent: **Responsive**: 1 full-width dashboard → 2 half-width → 3 third-width → 4 quarter-width → scrollable at 5+
RowTranslations.translatedContent: **Responsivo**: 1 dashboard largura total → 2 meia largura → 3 um terço da largura → 4 um quarto da largura → rolável a partir de 5+

RowTranslations.originContent: If no numbered variables are configured, falls back to **legacy single-instance mode**:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Se nenhuma variável numerada estiver configurada, retorna ao **modo legado de instância única**:

Content:
```yaml
environment:
  - GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL=http://gluetun:8000  # legacy
  - GLUETUN_API_KEY=token
```
Translation:
```yaml
environment:
  - GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL=http://gluetun:8000  # legacy
  - GLUETUN_API_KEY=token
```

Content:
### Per-Instance Authentication

Each instance can have different authentication:
Translation:
### Autenticação por Instância

Cada instância pode ter autenticação diferente:

RowTranslations.originContent: Each instance can have different authentication:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Cada instância pode ter autenticação diferente:

Content:
```yaml
# Instance with API key
- GLUETUN_1_API_KEY=my-secret-token

# Instance with HTTP Basic auth
- GLUETUN_2_USER=admin
- GLUETUN_2_PASSWORD=mysecret

# Instance with no auth
- GLUETUN_3_URL=http://gluetun-3:8000  # auth optional
```
Translation:
```yaml
# Instance with API key
- GLUETUN_1_API_KEY=my-secret-token

# Instance with HTTP Basic auth
- GLUETUN_2_USER=admin
- GLUETUN_2_PASSWORD=mysecret

# Instance with no auth
- GLUETUN_3_URL=http://gluetun-3:8000  # auth optional
```

Content:
---

## Configuration

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GLUETUN_1_*` to `GLUETUN_20_*` | _(empty)_ | **Multi-instance config** (up to 20 instances) |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_URL` | – | Gluetun HTTP control server URL for instance N |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_NAME` | `Instance {N}` | Display name for instance N |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Bearer token for instance N (if auth enabled) |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_USER` | _(empty)_ | Username for HTTP Basic auth (instance N) |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_PASSWORD` | _(empty)_ | Password for HTTP Basic auth (instance N) |
| `GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL` | `http://gluetun:8000` | **Legacy** – single instance only (fallback if no `GLUETUN_1_*` vars) |
| `GLUETUN_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | **Legacy** – Bearer token for single instance |
| `GLUETUN_USER` | _(empty)_ | **Legacy** – Username for HTTP Basic auth |
| `GLUETUN_PASSWORD` | _(empty)_ | **Legacy** – Password for HTTP Basic auth |
| `PORT` | `3000` | Port the web UI listens on |
| `TRUST_PROXY` | `false` | Set to `true` if running behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, etc.) |

---

## Security

- Port is bound to `127.0.0.1` — not exposed to the network
- Container runs as non-root with read-only filesystem and dropped capabilities
- Rate limiting applied to all API routes
- Upstream error details are logged server-side only — generic messages returned to the browser

### Reverse-proxy configuration

If you run gluetun-webui behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, Caddy, etc.), set `TRUST_PROXY=true` in your environment variables:
Translation:
---

## Configuração

| Variável | Padrão | Descrição |
|---|---|---|
| `GLUETUN_1_*` até `GLUETUN_20_*` | _(vazio)_ | **Configuração multi-instância** (até 20 instâncias) |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_URL` | – | URL do servidor de controle HTTP do Gluetun para a instância N |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_NAME` | `Instance {N}` | Nome de exibição para a instância N |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_API_KEY` | _(vazio)_ | Token Bearer para a instância N (se autenticação ativada) |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_USER` | _(vazio)_ | Usuário para autenticação HTTP Basic (instância N) |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_PASSWORD` | _(vazio)_ | Senha para autenticação HTTP Basic (instância N) |
| `GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL` | `http://gluetun:8000` | **Legado** – apenas uma instância (fallback se não houver variáveis `GLUETUN_1_*`) |
| `GLUETUN_API_KEY` | _(vazio)_ | **Legado** – Token Bearer para uma única instância |
| `GLUETUN_USER` | _(vazio)_ | **Legado** – Usuário para autenticação HTTP Basic |
| `GLUETUN_PASSWORD` | _(vazio)_ | **Legado** – Senha para autenticação HTTP Basic |
| `PORT` | `3000` | Porta em que a interface web escuta |
| `TRUST_PROXY` | `false` | Defina como `true` se rodar atrás de um proxy reverso (nginx, Traefik, etc.) |

---

## Segurança

- A porta está vinculada a `127.0.0.1` — não exposta à rede
- O container roda como não-root, com sistema de arquivos somente leitura e capacidades reduzidas
- Limitação de taxa aplicada a todas as rotas da API
- Detalhes de erros upstream são registrados apenas no servidor — mensagens genéricas retornadas ao navegador

### Configuração do proxy reverso

Se você executar o gluetun-webui atrás de um proxy reverso (nginx, Traefik, Caddy, etc.), defina `TRUST_PROXY=true` nas suas variáveis de ambiente:

RowTranslations.translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_URL` | – | URL do servidor de controle HTTP do Gluetun para a instância N |

RowTranslations.originContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_URL` | – | Gluetun HTTP control server URL for instance N |
RowTranslations.translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_NAME` | `Instance {N}` | Nome de exibição para a instância N |
| `GLUETUN_{N}_API_KEY` | _(vazio)_ | Token Bearer para a instância N (se autenticação ativada) |

RowTranslations.originContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Bearer token for instance N (if auth enabled) |
RowTranslations.translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_USER` | _(vazio)_ | Usuário para autenticação HTTP Basic (instância N) |

RowTranslations.originContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_USER` | _(empty)_ | Username for HTTP Basic auth (instance N) |
RowTranslations.translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_PASSWORD` | _(vazio)_ | Senha para autenticação HTTP Basic (instância N) |

RowTranslations.originContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_PASSWORD` | _(empty)_ | Password for HTTP Basic auth (instance N) |
RowTranslations.translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL` | `http://gluetun:8000` | **Legado** – apenas uma instância (fallback se não houver variáveis `GLUETUN_1_*`) |

RowTranslations.originContent: | `GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL` | `http://gluetun:8000` | **Legacy** – single instance only (fallback if no `GLUETUN_1_*` vars) |
RowTranslations.translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_API_KEY` | _(vazio)_ | **Legado** – Token Bearer para uma única instância |
| `PORT` | `3000` | Porta em que a interface web escuta |

RowTranslations.originContent: | `PORT` | `3000` | Port the web UI listens on |
RowTranslations.translatedContent: | `TRUST_PROXY` | `false` | Defina como `true` se rodar atrás de um proxy reverso (nginx, Traefik, etc.) |

RowTranslations.originContent: | `TRUST_PROXY` | `false` | Set to `true` if running behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, etc.) |

RowTranslations.translatedContent: - A porta está vinculada a `127.0.0.1` — não exposta à rede

RowTranslations.originContent: - Port is bound to `127.0.0.1` — not exposed to the network
RowTranslations.translatedContent: - O container roda como não-root, com sistema de arquivos somente leitura e capacidades reduzidas

RowTranslations.originContent: - Container runs as non-root with read-only filesystem and dropped capabilities

RowTranslations.translatedContent: Se você executar o gluetun-webui atrás de um proxy reverso (nginx, Traefik, Caddy, etc.), defina `TRUST_PROXY=true` nas suas variáveis de ambiente:

RowTranslations.originContent: If you run gluetun-webui behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, Caddy, etc.), set `TRUST_PROXY=true` in your environment variables:

Content:
```yaml
gluetun-webui:
  image: scuzza/gluetun-webui:latest
  environment:
    - GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL=http://gluetun:8000
    - TRUST_PROXY=true  # Required for reverse proxies
```
Translation:
```yaml
gluetun-webui:
  image: scuzza/gluetun-webui:latest
  environment:
    - GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL=http://gluetun:8000
    - TRUST_PROXY=true  # Required for reverse proxies
```

Content:
This allows the app to correctly parse `X-Forwarded-For` and related headers for accurate rate limiting and IP detection. **Note:** Only enable this if you're actually behind a reverse proxy, as it trusts proxy headers from your reverse proxy.

### Reverse-proxy authentication

The VPN start/stop controls have no built-in authentication. If you expose the UI beyond localhost, place it behind a reverse proxy with HTTP Basic auth.

**Caddy** (`Caddyfile`):
Translation:
Isso permite que o aplicativo analise corretamente `X-Forwarded-For` e cabeçalhos relacionados para limitar taxas e detectar IPs de forma precisa. **Nota:** Só ative isso se você realmente estiver atrás de um proxy reverso, pois ele confia nos cabeçalhos de proxy do seu proxy reverso.

### Autenticação de proxy reverso

Os controles de iniciar/parar VPN não possuem autenticação embutida. Se você expuser a interface além do localhost, coloque-a atrás de um proxy reverso com autenticação HTTP Basic.

**Caddy** (`Caddyfile`):

Content:

**Nginx** (`nginx.conf`):
Translation:

**Nginx** (`nginx.conf`):

Content:
Generate a password file with: `htpasswd -c /etc/nginx/.htpasswd user`

**Traefik** (Docker labels):
Translation:
Gere um arquivo de senha com: `htpasswd -c /etc/nginx/.htpasswd user`

**Traefik** (rótulos Docker):

RowTranslations.originContent: Generate a password file with: `htpasswd -c /etc/nginx/.htpasswd user`
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Gere um arquivo de senha com: `htpasswd -c /etc/nginx/.htpasswd user`

Content:
```yaml
labels:
  - "traefik.enable=true"
  - "traefik.http.routers.gluetun-webui.rule=Host(`your.domain.com`)"
  - "traefik.http.routers.gluetun-webui.middlewares=auth"
  - "traefik.http.middlewares.auth.basicauth.users=user:$$apr1$$<hash>"
```
Translation:
```yaml
labels:
  - "traefik.enable=true"
  - "traefik.http.routers.gluetun-webui.rule=Host(`your.domain.com`)"
  - "traefik.http.routers.gluetun-webui.middlewares=auth"
  - "traefik.http.middlewares.auth.basicauth.users=user:$$apr1$$<hash>"
```

Content:
Generate a hash with: `htpasswd -nb user password`
